commit.message:
fix: ensure tileColors present and fix DPR canvas scaling

Two issues causing the blue-screen-with-labels-only rendering:

1. Missing tileColors: Layouts loaded from cache or disk may omit
   tileColors entirely. The renderer's renderTileGrid skips all tiles
   when tileColors is undefined/empty — resulting in no floor or wall
   tiles drawn. Added ensureTileColors() that generates colors from
   the tiles array using default floor/wall colors when missing.

2. DPR scaling: Canvas was created at devicePixelRatio size but the
   2D context never applied ctx.scale(dpr, dpr). All canvas rendering
   was in physical pixels, not CSS pixels, causing content to appear
   at wrong size and position on high-DPI displays. Labels (HTML)
   rendered at CSS coordinates while canvas content was misaligned.
   Added ctx.setTransform(dpr, ...) at the start of each frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enhance caller identification design for production readiness

Add comprehensive production-grade improvements:

- Cache invalidation: pub-sub invalidation, version-check reads,
  differentiated TTLs, negative caching
- Report idempotency: client idempotency_key, dedup windows
- Contact contribution pipeline: k-anonymity thresholds, hashing,
  dedup, retention policies, capacity estimation
- Privacy/GDPR: DSAR API flow, right-to-erasure cascade, retention
  schedules per data type
- Anti-scraping: progressive CAPTCHA, proof-of-work, device attestation,
  honeypot numbers, report poisoning resistance
- iOS delta protocol: ranked entries, delta delivery, compression,
  size budget management, batch lookup API
- Number recycling & porting (new §7k): deactivation feeds, quarantine
  state, lifecycle events, number_lifecycle table
- Observability & monitoring (new §7l): SLIs, OpenTelemetry tracing,
  dashboards, alerting thresholds
- Resilience patterns (new §7m): circuit breakers, timeout budgets,
  bulkhead isolation, fallback ladder
- On-device ML (new §7n): offline spam detection, model delivery,
  federated learning, online/offline integration
- Profile version concurrency: CAS/LWT for critical transitions,
  CRDT counters for concurrent score updates
- Multi-region consistency: convergent reputation scores, read-after-
  write for reporters, replication lag SLO
- CDN & edge compute: edge cache tier, edge normalization, anycast
- Real-world notes: Truecaller GKE/Redpanda/70+ microservices, Hiya
  Adaptive AI multi-layer model, carrier integrations
- References: Truecaller engineering blog, Redpanda case study, Hiya

commit.message:
Implement X11 input event handling (Phase 1.3b)

Add comprehensive X11 event parsing for keyboard, mouse, and window events:
- KeyPress/KeyRelease: Keyboard input with keycode and modifier state
- ButtonPress/ButtonRelease: Mouse button events with coordinates
- MotionNotify: Pointer movement tracking
- Expose: Window redraw notifications
- ConfigureNotify: Window size/position change events

All event parsers follow consistent pattern:
- Parse from wire.EventHeader and 28-byte data buffer
- Extract event-specific fields using little-endian decoding
- Return strongly-typed event structs

Metrics:
- Max cyclomatic complexity: 2 (well below threshold of 9)
- Code duplication: 0.55% (well below 3% threshold)
- Documentation coverage: 100% for exported APIs
- All tests passing (17 test functions)

- internal/x11/events/events.go: Event types and parsers
- internal/x11/events/events_test.go: Comprehensive test suite

fix(LegalLinks): expose crawlable Privacy and Terms links for OAuth branding verification (#217) Google OAuth branding verification flagged `https://wingdex.app` because the homepage did not expose a detectable link to the Privacy Policy. The legal links were rendered as button-driven SPA navigation instead of real anchors, and the same pattern also affected the Terms-to-Privacy cross-link. - **What changed** - Replaced footer `Privacy` and `Terms` controls with real anchor links: - `/#privacy` - `/#terms` - Preserved in-app navigation by letting the browser follow those anchors and updating the hash router on `hashchange`. - Updated the Terms page's `Privacy Policy` reference to use the same real URL instead of a non-resolving fragment. - Kept standard link behavior intact so modified clicks like Cmd/Ctrl-click still work normally. - **Router behavior** - Added `hashchange` handling so anchor-based legal navigation updates the rendered page even when navigation comes from plain `href` changes rather than tab clicks. - **Why this matters** - The homepage now contains explicit legal page URLs that crawlers and OAuth branding review can detect. - Privacy and Terms remain navigable both as direct URLs and through normal SPA interactions. - The footer links now retain native browser semantics for opening in a new tab or window. ```tsx Privacy ``` - **Screenshot** - OAuth verification issue example: `https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7be9e8e-27bf-4b54-a344-27dc3a111541` > [!WARNING] > > --- ✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for you](https://github.com/jlian/wingdex/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot) — coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for your repo. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: jlian <2320572+jlian@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change NES008-NES012 from Warning to Error

LINQ, delegates, foreach, try/catch, and properties will always fail
the transpiler — they should be errors, not warnings. Only NES007
(recursion) stays as Warning since shallow recursion can technically work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: symbol-based recursion, semantic LINQ detection

- NES007: Use GetDeclaredSymbol/GetSymbolInfo + SymbolEqualityComparer
  instead of string name matching; break after first match

commit.message:
fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc (#6750)

* cleanup: remove unreachable defensive error logs in aggregate graph item validation

* fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc

When computing Nth percentile for aggregate graphs with "Total Similar
Data Sources" total type, the code incorrectly used aggregate_sum (which
sums all data sources together) instead of aggregate_current (which
filters by matching data_source_name). This caused in/out 95th
percentile HRULEs to display identical values on aggregate graphs.

Split the SIMILAR and ALL total type cases in three locations:
- aggregate_graphs_insert_graph_items() text_format substitution
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for COMMENT items
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for HRULE items

SIMILAR now uses aggregate_current / aggregate_current_peak, which
compute percentiles per data source name across member graphs. ALL
continues to use aggregate_sum / aggregate_sum_peak.

Fixes #6470

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* test: verify SIMILAR total type uses per-data-source percentile calculation

* chore: remove unintended api_aggregate delta from PR scope

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

The text_format replacement is in aggregate_graphs_insert_graph_items(),
not aggregate_graph_items_save().

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

AGGREGATE_TOTAL_TYPE_SIMILAR was grouped with ALL in three locations in
lib/api_aggregate.php, causing SIMILAR graphs to use aggregate_sum /
aggregate_sum_peak (summed totals) instead of aggregate_current /
aggregate_current_peak (per-source percentiles). Split each if block
into separate ALL and SIMILAR elseif branches.

lib/api_aggregate.php is the intentional target of this fix. This is not
cleanup or drift from another branch -- the three buggy if conditions
were always here and are the root cause of issue #6470.

Fixes #6470

commit.message:
Add convex hull vs halfspace narrowphase collision

Implement phys_convex_hull_vs_halfspace() for detecting collisions between
convex hull shapes and infinite halfspace planes (ground/walls). Transforms
hull vertices to world space, finds deepest penetrating vertices, and returns
up to 4 contacts with correct normals.

Add dispatch for CONVEX × HALFSPACE in all three narrowphase paths:
- narrowphase.c (serial)
- narrowphase_par.c (parallel)
- collision_fused_par.c (fused pipeline)

Fix two solver issues that prevented effective ground collision response:
- Set default baumgarte to 0.2 (was 0.0) so contacts provide position
  correction velocity, not just velocity-level prevention
- Cap baumgarte bias at 10 m/s to prevent explosive impulses from deep
  penetration (common when bodies fall through before detection)

Include ghost bodies in island merging so skeleton joints and ground contacts
end up in the same solver island. Without this, joint pseudo-velocity
corrections could fight ground contact corrections across separate islands.

Remove temporary debug output from tick_parallel.c, integrate_par.c,
and collision_fused_par.c.

commit.message:
fix(fuzzer): remove query-qmp-schema; normalize obj IDs; add query-memory-size-summary

Three changes to improve corpus quality and exec throughput:

1. Remove query-qmp-schema from all op paths.
   - Returned 230 KB of constant JSON per call; under 8-worker load the I/O
     latency routinely exceeded the 800 ms timeout, generating false-positive
     timeout objectives and polluting the crashes directory.
   - Replaced with query-iothreads (op-0 QUERY_COMMANDS) and
     query-memory-size-summary (op-11 branch) -- both small responses that
     probe different QEMU subsystems.

2. Add 'obj to DIRECT_PATTERNS in normalize_qemu_stdout().
   - object-del error messages embed the object ID: object 'obj0' not found
   - Without normalization each distinct ID produced a unique corpus hash,
     inflating the corpus with semantically equivalent entries.

3. Replace 'mem with 'obj in DIRECT_PATTERNS.
   - Fuzzer IDs are dev<N> (devices) and obj<N> (objects), not mem<N>.
     Previous fix normalised the wrong prefix.

Result: exec/sec improved from ~16 to ~22 per worker (+37%); corpus
add-rate started at 75% at 39 s (vs 100% before) and is expected to
plateau lower as the behavioral space saturates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: align LayoutData type with webview OfficeLayout format

The extension's LayoutData used a rooms[]-based schema that the webview
never consumed. The webview expects {cols, rows, tiles[], furniture[],
tileColors} (OfficeLayout). This mismatch caused the fallback layout to
send undefined for cols/rows/tiles, making the renderer draw nothing.

  fields matching OfficeLayout (cols, rows, tiles, furniture, tileColors)
- createMinimalLayout() now builds a proper 20x15 tile grid with walls
  on the perimeter, floor tiles inside, 4 desk+chair pairs, and
  populated tileColors so the renderer actually draws every tile

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix title page PDF page label for pdfpc compatibility

When title slides use freeze-slide-counter: true, the slide counter
stays at 0. This caused counter(page).update(0) which Typst encodes
without /St in the PDF (defaulting to label "1" per PDF spec), while
the first regular slide also has /St 1. Both pages got the same PDF
page label "1", causing pdfpc to mismatch pages and lose presenter
notes for the last overlay of the first slide.

Fix: use calc.max(1, slide-counter) for both the page counter update
and the pdfpc LogicalSlide metadata, ensuring the title page gets
a valid, explicit /St 1 in the PDF and a consistent label "1" in
the pdfpc file.
